knownIssues.title: Link Box can fail and cause intermittent disconnects
knownIssues.description: The HTC Link Box that converts the headset tether to DisplayPort and USB is a known failure point. It can cause random disconnects, audio dropouts, and display issues.
knownIssues.workaround: Ensure Link Box firmware is up to date via SteamVR. Replace the Link Box if disconnects persist — third-party Link Box alternatives are available.

knownIssues.title: Built-in headphones have audible background hiss
knownIssues.description: The Vive Pro's integrated headphones exhibit a persistent high-frequency hiss, especially noticeable in quiet in-game environments.
knownIssues.workaround: Use a 3.5mm headphone adapter to connect external headphones. Alternatively, use SteamVR audio settings to route audio to a different output device.

knownIssues.title: Lighthouse base stations require line-of-sight sync
knownIssues.description: SteamVR 1.0 base stations use optical sync and must have line of sight to each other. Physical obstruction between base stations causes tracking failures.
knownIssues.workaround: Mount base stations diagonally in the room at 2m+ height. Ensure no furniture blocks the optical sync beam. Upgrade to SteamVR 2.0 base stations for radio-sync (no line of sight required).

optimizationTips.tip: Enable supersampling for sharper visuals at 90Hz
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Turning up the picture quality in SteamVR makes the image noticeably clearer on the Vive Pro.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: The Vive Pro's 1440x1600 per eye benefits from 1.3-1.5x supersampling in SteamVR to reduce aliasing visible through the Fresnel lenses. Set per-application resolution in SteamVR settings. GTX 1080 Ti or better handles 150% SS at 90Hz comfortably.

optimizationTips.tip: Use SteamVR motion smoothing for demanding games
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Turn on SteamVR's frame smoothing feature so the game stays fluid even when your GPU is under heavy load.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: SteamVR motion smoothing synthesizes frames when GPU rendering misses the 90Hz deadline, inserting a motion-predicted frame. Enable per-game in SteamVR Video settings. Most effective when the GPU hits 45fps+ consistently — below that, judder appears.

optimizationTips.tip: Run SteamVR in Direct Display Mode
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Direct mode bypasses Windows desktop rendering, reducing the delay between your head movements and what you see.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: SteamVR Direct Display Mode bypasses the Windows DWM compositor. The Vive Pro should not appear as a monitor in Windows Display Settings. Verify by checking SteamVR → Settings → Developer → Direct Display Mode. Eliminates 1-2 frames of compositor latency.
